title: tilia cordata subsp. nasczokinii
intro: Tilia cordata subsp. nasczokinii, commonly known as the Chinese small-leaved linden, is a subspecies of the small-leaved linden tree. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues due to its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and sedative properties. The therapeutic actions of Tilia cordata subsp. nasczokinii include relaxation, sleep promotion, and relief from anxiety and stress. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
benefits: Tilia cordata subsp. nasczokinii has many benefits, such as being a natural remedy for various health issues, including reducing stress and anxiety by promoting relaxation, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of insomnia. Its anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe skin irritations, reduce joint pain, and even alleviate symptoms of arthritis, making it an effective treatment for various health conditions. Furthermore, this plant has been shown to possess antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage, improve cardiovascular health, and even support the immune system. By incorporating Tilia cordata subsp. nasczokinii into one's daily life, individuals can experience improved overall well-being, enhanced mood, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases.
constituents: Tilia cordata subsp. nasczokinii has many active constituents, such as flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin, phenolic acids like gallic acid and ellagic acid, and terpenoids like limonene and beta-pinene. These compounds have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, improve respiratory health, and reduce the risk of chronic diseases like heart disease and cancer. The medicinal constituents of Tilia cordata subsp. nasczokinii also include triterpenoids like ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, and can help to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. By incorporating Tilia cordata subsp. nasczokinii into their daily lives, people may experience improved overall health and well-being, and enjoy a better quality of life.
parts: Tilia cordata subsp. nasczokinii has many medicinal parts, such as its leaves, flowers, and bark. The leaves of Tilia cordata subsp. nasczokinii contain flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flowers of this plant are a rich source of volatile oils, including linalool and limonene, which are used to treat anxiety and insomnia. The bark of Tilia cordata subsp. nasczokinii contains salicin, a compound that is similar to aspirin and has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
side_effects: Tilia cordata subsp. nasczokinii can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal problems, including nausea and vomiting, due to its high content of tannins and flavonoids. Additionally, consuming excessive amounts of this plant may cause allergic reactions, such as skin rashes and respiratory issues. In severe cases, improper use of Tilia cordata subsp. nasczokinii can lead to liver damage and kidney problems. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using this plant for medicinal purposes to avoid any potential side effects.
